hero.name: Frequencies
hero.tagline: A small band of tech podcasts we keep in the rotation
hero.description: Shows we listen to at the StoicSoft studio while commuting, walking, or pairing on a problem. Hand-picked, non-sponsored, honestly annotated.

items.name: Signal FM
items.description: Our own show — a weekly long conversation with builders inside the StoicSoft studio’s orbit. Hosted by Clara and Dante.

items.name: log.stoicsoft.com · audio
items.description: The StoicSoft engineering blog, narrated by the authors. A good fit for the commute or a long walk. Publishes a day after the written post.

items.name: Shipcraft Lectures
items.tagline: The audio side of the Shipcraft cohort
items.description: Jonas Park’s lectures from the Shipcraft founder cohort, released weekly during active semesters and archived the rest of the year.

items.name: Field & Form Recordings
items.description: Selected essays from Field & Form, the StoicSoft quarterly, recorded as long-form audio. Produced with care — studio mic, no ads.

items.name: ServerCompass Pager
items.tagline: On-call war stories from real teams
items.description: Short interviews with SRE leads about the alerts that woke them up and what they did next. Built around the ServerCompass monitoring perspective.

items.name: Routine Mornings
items.description: A daily ten-minute podcast from the Routine app team. One prompt, one thought, delivered at 6am. Listen while the kettle boils.

items.name: Atelier Table
items.tagline: Studio conversations, one at a time
items.description: One-studio, one-episode conversations with founders of the studios on our Atelier directory. Inside their process, without the press gloss.

items.name: Halo Quiet Hour
items.description: A weekly hour of ambient sound and slow reflection from the Halo habit app. The anti-podcast podcast.

items.name: Aether Retrospectives
items.tagline: Engineering post-mortems from the Aether team
items.description: Aether’s engineering retrospectives — what we shipped, what we got wrong, what we changed. Honest to a fault.

items.name: Indie Open Office
items.tagline: Live-coached founders solving their real problem
items.description: Once a month, an indie founder brings a real problem to a one-hour live coaching session with a StoicSoft curator. Raw, helpful.

items.name: Open Roots Field Notes
items.description: Quarterly field recordings from the Open Roots replanting crews — saplings, soil, the wind in the Alentejo. Produced as audio documentary.

items.name: Mei Wong · Livecoding
items.tagline: A StoicSoft engineer, coding out loud
items.description: Mei Wong building small things in real time — Next.js, Postgres, and the occasional Rust detour. Lightly edited, timestamps in every episode.
